task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the app/build.gradle.kts file open in the editor with an implementation line for 'org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-android' (likely version 1.7.3 or newer).

task_completion.description: Convert ArticleRepository methods to suspend functions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ArticleRepository.kt open in the editor. The methods (e.g., fetchByCategory, search, saveArticle) should now have the 'suspend' keyword before 'fun'.

task_completion.description: Update FeedActivity to use lifecycleScope.launch and withContext(Dispatchers.IO)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for FeedActivity.kt open in the editor. The code should show 'lifecycleScope.launch' and 'withContext(Dispatchers.IO)' being used for background operations instead of the old 'FetchArticlesTask().execute()' or 'SaveArticleTask().execute()'.

task_completion.description: Update SearchActivity to use lifecycleScope.launch and withContext(Dispatchers.IO)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SearchActivity.kt open in the editor. The code should show 'lifecycleScope.launch' and 'withContext(Dispatchers.IO)' being used instead of 'SearchArticlesTask().execute()'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the project compiles successfully after the migration
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Build' tool window at the bottom of Android Studio showing 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or a green checkmark indicating the Gradle build finished without errors.

integrity.description: Agent used Android Studio to edit files and build the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Android Studio IDE interface being used to edit the Kotlin and Gradle files, rather than just terminal text editors like nano or vim.

integrity.description: Build success is from an actual Gradle execution
integrity.visual_evidence: The build success should be visible in the Android Studio Build/Run tool window or a genuine terminal running './gradlew build', not a mocked text file.

integrity.description: Modifications were made to the correct FeedReaderApp project
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths visible in the IDE tabs or project explorer should correspond to '/home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/FeedReaderApp/app/src/main/java/com/example/feedreader'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/FeedReaderApp. The target package is com.example.feedreader. The original code uses deprecated AsyncTask classes (FetchArticlesTask, SaveArticleTask, SearchArticlesTask, LoadSavedTask) which are called via .execute() in FeedActivity and SearchActivity. The ArticleRepository methods originally use Thread.sleep() to simulate blocking I/O. A successful migration will show 'suspend' added to the repository methods, and the Activities using 'lifecycleScope.launch' with 'withContext(Dispatchers.IO)' to call these repository methods directly, bypassing or removing the AsyncTask classes entirely.
